title: "Dual monitors, windows+messages appear on the wrong one"
date: 2007-10-26
forum: General Help

### Post by thelost on 2007-10-26
I have dual monitors and have successfully got twinview working using someone elses xorg.conf, however windows are appearing on my second monitor, as are messages from OSDs/etc. It's pretty annoying.

here is my xorg.conf

```

# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Wed Sep 12 14:30:30 PDT 2007

# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	
	# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
	#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  screen 0 "Screen0" 0 0
	Inputdevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	Inputdevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load		"glx"
	Load		"dbe"
	Load		"vnc"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option		"Xinerama"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"	"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"	"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Horizsync	30.0	-	70.0
	Vertrefresh	50.0	-	160.0
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Monitor0"
	Vendorname	"Unknown"
	Modelname	"Mitsubishi electron19b"
	Horizsync	30.0	-	95.0
	Vertrefresh	50.0	-	152.0
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"nVidia Corporation G80 [GeForce 8800 GTS]"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	Option		"AddARGBVisuals"	"True"
	Option		"AddARGBGLXVisuals"	"True"
	Option		"NoLogo"	"True"
	Busid		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Videocard0"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	Vendorname	"NVIDIA Corporation"
	Boardname	"GeForce 8800 GTS"
	Option		"TwinViewXineramaInfoOrder"	"DFP, CRT"
	Option		"UseDisplayDevice"	"DFP, CRT"
	Option		"TwinViewOrientation"	"DFP LeftOf CRT"
EndSection
Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"nVidia Corporation G80 [GeForce 8800 GTS]"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	Defaultdepth	24
	Option		"SecurityTypes"	"VncAuth"
	Option		"UserPasswdVerifier"	"VncAuth"
	Option		"PasswordFile"	"/root/.vnc/passwd"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Screen0"
	Device		"Videocard0"
	Monitor		"Monitor0"
	Defaultdepth	24
	Option		"TwinView"	"1"
	Option		"metamodes"	"DFP: 1280x1024_60 +1280+0, CRT: 1280x1024 +0+0; DFP: nvidia-auto-select +1024+0, CRT: 1024x768 +0+0; DFP: nvidia-auto-select +800+0, CRT: 800x600 +0+0; DFP: nvidia-auto-select +640+0, CRT: 640x480 +0+0"
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Modes		"1600x1200"	"1280x1024"	"1024x768"	"800x600"	"640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

```

What do I need to change so all windows open on the correct screen and all OSD messages from things like Amarok/Thunderbird show on my main screen and not the secondary?

---

### Post by kingjere on 2007-10-26
I am not certain, but I think this has much less to do with Xorg than it does the desktop environment. For example, for amarok at least the OSD is configurable via the menu. You can put it anywhere you want. What I would research were I you is how gnome or kde (whichever) handles window placement. Also there may be a way to make applications remember where they are supposed to open depending on your desktop environment.

PS I dont want to assume you use one or the other because you  did mention amarok.

---

### Post by thelost on 2007-10-27
amarok was perhaps a bad example.

As far as I know Thunderbird does not allow you to move its alert window, which always appears on my second window.

Can anyone explain the important parts of xorg.conf as shown above that describe how the monitors are set up?

Once I have an idea how the twinview settings work I can tell at least if they are correct!
